flashcards.definition: Shared resources depleted when individuals act in self-interest; solutions include regulation, privatization, and community management.
Economic tool comparing costs and benefits of a project or policy; often fails to account for environmental externalities.
A cost or benefit not reflected in market prices (e.g., pollution costs borne by society, not the polluter).
When potential for harm exists but science is uncertain, take preventive action to avoid environmental damage.
Flexible resource management approach that adjusts strategies based on monitoring and new data.
